what_it_does: This file coordinates two parallel loops — a guided-review loop [M] and a cognition loop [C] — that jointly build a registry-generation pipeline. It maps tasks RG1–RG11 to file-disjoint owners, defines the schema and cascade seams where the loops meet, and tells a dedicated cron how to fire one buildable criterion per cycle or record a blocked state.
observations: Tasks RG1–RG11 are split across [M], [C], [J], and [C/J] lanes with explicit file ownership.
RG7 is a cascade spec authored by [M] and run by [C]'s engine, serving as the main meeting point.
Two schema contracts are frozen: candidates.json between RG1 and RG3, and the confirmed-set dossier between RG6 and RG9.
The roles/ directory is declared a [C]-only seam; [M] does not write roles/*.
RG10 'whole-by-use' is framed as the convergence-round proof that a once-dead element resolves.
The cron is instructed to build one buildable [M] criterion per fire, or record blocked if waiting on [C] or the cascade seam.
The file references an external PROTOCOL, Tim 2026-06-09, the V-A chain, swarm, gate-green, flag-tiers, and shared-main/claims-board concepts.
standouts: Explicit file-disjoint parallel split between [M] and [C] loops.
RG7 cascade spec as the co-authored, run-by-engine integration seam.
RG10 whole-by-use as the operational convergence proof.
roles/ ownership boundary: only [C] writes roles.
Cron protocol of one gated criterion per fire plus blocked-state recording.
conventions: Uses a markdown table to map task ID, description, lane, and files.
Labels lanes with bracketed single-letter codes ([M], [C], [J], [C/J]).
Heavy use of shared-protocol vocabulary: gate-green, flag-tier, claims-board, shared-main, convergence round.
References external schemas and roles rather than defining them inline.
File-local coordination doc that also embeds cron firing instructions.
concerns: Dense jargon and external references (PROTOCOL, V-A chain, swarm) are not defined inside this file.
RG2 says [M] reuses [C]'s role, creating a lane-ownership nuance that is only briefly noted.
RG11 'GENERALIZE (live surface)' has no files and a vague forward scope.
Relies on schemas and role implementations that live outside this file, so the contracts are not self-contained.
The 2026-06-09 date is future-looking relative to typical knowledge cutoffs, which may indicate a speculative or staged plan.
notes: A coordination contract that splits registry-generation work between two autonomous loops and pins them together at schemas, a cascade spec, and a use-based convergence proof.
questions: What is the exact content of the referenced PROTOCOL?
What are the precise field-level schemas for candidates.json and the confirmed-set dossier?
How is the run:// step wiring in RG7 specified and executed?
What do 'V-A chain' and 'swarm' specifically mean in this context?
What is the operational definition of 'whole-by-use' verification in RG10?
How are gate-green criteria and flag-tiers evaluated?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose — coordinate parallel [M] and [C] loops for registry generation — matches the content: a task split table, seam contracts, and cron firing rules.
apparent_themes: Strict role and file ownership boundaries
intention_signals: It is trying to create a safe, conflict-free way for two autonomous loops to co-build a registry-generation pipeline by freezing interfaces, splitting file ownership, and proving success through a shared cascade spec and a use-based convergence round.
novelty: The explicit 'two parallel loops, file-disjoint, meeting at a cascade spec' pattern and the 'whole-by-use' convergence proof rather than static validation.
contribution: This file contributes a clear ownership map and seam-contract vocabulary for parallel registry-generation work, which a fused system would keep to understand which loop owns which files and where the integration points are.
summary_for_embedding: Coordination document that assigns file-disjoint registry-generation tasks (RG1–RG11) between guided-review [M] and cognition [C] loops, defines schema and cascade seams, and prescribes a dedicated cron to build one gated criterion per cycle toward a whole-by-use convergence round.
intention_for_embedding: To enable two parallel loops to safely co-build an address registry generation pipeline by freezing schemas, splitting file ownership, and converging at a shared cascade spec verified by use.
